combat sexual and domestic violence against American Indian and Alaska Native women

topic.name: Election Reform and Representation
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at reforming election processes and enhancing representation in government.

Document 1: Requires all candidates for election to the House of Representatives to run in a single open primary, regardless of political party preference.
Requires the Comptroller General (GAO) to study the feasibility and desirability of enacting national standards and criteria for congressional redistricting.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more.
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 3: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives and in the Senate.
Increases membership of the House from 435 to 436 Members.
Document 4: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act to require the Board of Elections and Ethics to hold a special election for filling vacancies.
Eliminates the specific alternative of a special election on the same day as the next general election.
Document 5: Treats the District of Columbia as a state for purposes of representation in the House of Representatives and in the Senate.
Increases membership of the House from 435 to 436 Members.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more.
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 7: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition regarding collective bargaining representation.
Requires the NLRB to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable but not before 35 calendar days after the filing of the election petition.
Document 8: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to decide whether the unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit before the election.
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.
Document 9: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition.
Requires the Board to acquire from the employer a list of all employees eligible to vote in the election.
Document 10: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to require that no investigative hearing take place until at least 14 days after the filing of an election petition.
Requires the Board to acquire from the employer a list of all employees eligible to vote in the election.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on grocery bags of nonwoven fabric

Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at enhancing data privacy, security, and accountability in the handling of personally identifiable information.

Document 1: Imposes a fine and/or prison term for concealing a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information
Requires business entities to implement a comprehensive program for data privacy and security
Document 2: Requires the FTC to promulgate regulations requiring security policies for electronic data containing personal information
Requires information brokers to establish procedures to verify the accuracy of collected information
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act to protect against the acquisition of certain records without specific identifiers
Prohibits the federal government from acquiring records of electronic communication without specific identifiers
Document 4: Prohibits federal entities from compelling journalists to disclose protected information without judicial approval
Establishes a qualified privilege for journalists to withhold confidential information
Document 5: Places restrictions on the bulk collection of wire or electronic communications under FISA
Defines 'content' to exclude certain identifying information
Document 6: Establishes a new process for the FBI to apply for production of business records under FISA
Prohibits the FBI from applying for orders without specific selection terms
Document 7: Amends FISA to establish a new process for FBI applications regarding business records
Prohibits the FBI from applying for production orders without specific selection terms
Document 8: Establishes a new process for FBI applications for business records under FISA
Defines 'call detail record' and sets standards for production
Document 9: Amends FISA to establish a new process for business records production
Prohibits the FBI from applying for tangible thing production orders without specific selection terms
Document 10: Identifies risks to U.S. financial stability and promotes market discipline
Requires supervision for nonbank financial companies that may pose risks to financial stability

topic.name: Medicare Coverage Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at expanding and improving Medicare coverage for various healthcare services and providers.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to deem an individual receiving outpatient observation services in a hospital to be an inpatient
entitle the individual to Medicare coverage of any post-hospital extended care services
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide payment under Medicare part A on a reasonable cost basis
for anesthesia services furnished by a physician who is an anesthesiologist in certain rural hospitals
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide Medicare coverage for screening computed tomography as a colorectal screening test
exclude screening computed tomography colonography from the meaning of 'imaging services'
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians
to serve hospice patients
Document 5: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to extend to nurse practitioners, physician assistants, clinical nurse specialists, and certified nurse midwives payment for primary care services
at a rate not less than 100% of the Medicare payment rate applicable to physician for such services
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act, with respect to inpatient hospital service payments to certain hospitals
to exclude certain cancer hospitals from the meaning of subsection (d) hospital
Document 7: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide payment under Medicare part A on a reasonable cost basis
for anesthesia services furnished by a physician who is an anesthesiologist in certain rural hospitals
Document 8: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians
to serve hospice patients
Document 9: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for coverage of clinical pharmacist practitioner services under Medicare part B
Supplementary Medical Insurance
Document 10: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for coverage of clinical pharmacist practitioner services under Medicare part B
Supplementary Medical Insurance

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving and regulating the Medicare and Medicaid programs, focusing on payment rules, healthcare provider inclusion, and patient care management.

Document 1: This bill amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to modify payment rules and other provisions related to the Medicare and Medicaid programs.
The bill modifies provisions related to reimbursement under Medicare for certain drugs furnished through durable medical equipment (DME).
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to include as a health care provider behavioral and mental health professionals, substance abuse professionals, psychiatric hospitals, certain community mental health centers, and residential or outpatient mental health or substance abuse treatment facilities.
Makes eligible professionals under the Medicaid program certain clinical psychologists providing qualified psychologist services.
Document 4: This bill amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to establish an integrated chronic care delivery program through which qualified 'Better Care Programs' shall promote accountability and better care management for chronically ill patient populations.
The program shall focus on containing long-term costs and improving the overall health of the Medicare population.

topic.name: Food Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating food safety, particularly concerning harmful additives and contaminants in food packaging and cosmetics.

1: Prohibits the distribution of a food if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA)
Requires the Secretary to promote and facilitate the use of BPA replacements.
2: Treats as a banned hazardous substance under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act any children's product containing more than specified amounts of lead
Requires periodic review and, when technologically feasible, more stringent limits.
3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow a person to submit an application for licensure of a biological product based on its similarity to a licensed biological product
Sets forth application requirements, including that the application demonstrate that the biological is biosimilar to a reference product.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Agriculture
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. energy policy, including oil and gas leasing, renewable energy initiatives, and agricultural commodity programs.

Document 1: Prohibits oil and gas preleasing or leasing of any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) that was not available for oil and gas leasing as of July 1, 2008
Requires any oil and gas leasing of OCS areas to be conducted in accordance with the July 2, 1983, 'Memorandum of Agreement'
Document 2: Repeals, but continues for crop year 2013: (1) direct payments, (2) countercyclical payments
States that this subtitle shall be effective for crop years 2014-2018.
Document 3: Sustained, predictable federal funding is essential to U.S. leadership in science and technology
The NSF should commit itself fully to transparency and accountability.
Document 4: Repeals direct payments under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to notify farm owners regarding their opportunity to elect to retain base acres.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (Secretary) to adjust base acres for covered commodities for a farm
Requires election of either direct payments and counter-cyclical payments or conservation payments.
Document 6: Defines specified terms related to commodity programs
Requires election of either direct payments and counter-cyclical payments or conservation payments.
Document 7: Sets forth provisions concerning the reduction of global warming pollution, energy efficiency, renewable energy
Declares that it is U.S. policy to support the growth of voluntary renewable energy markets.
Document 8: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
Defines 'GHG emissions' to mean emissions of a GHG, including stationary combustion source emissions.
Document 9: Establishes in the Treasury the Clean Energy Investment Fund
Requires the Secretary of Energy to develop, publish for public review and comment, and review goals for the deployment of clean energy technologies.
Document 10: Sets forth provisions concerning clean energy, energy efficiency, reducing global warming pollution
Establishes a cap-and-trade system for greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions.

topic.name: Immigration Reform and Family Unity
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming immigration policies, particularly focusing on family reunification and pathways to legal status for certain groups of immigrants.

Document 1: Adjusted Residency for Military Service Act
cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional nonimmigrant status, an alien who entered the United States before his or her 16th birthday
Document 2: Reuniting Families Act
establish the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000
Document 3: Comprehensive Immigration Reform Act of 2011
creates a lawful prospective immigrant status for a qualifying alien
Document 4: Recognizing American Children Act
authorize the Department of Homeland Security to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional nonimmigrant status
Document 5: Reuniting Families Act
increases immigration visas for unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens
Document 6: Reuniting Families Act
establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000
Document 7: Reuniting Families Act
revises the definition of 'immediate relative'
Document 8: Reuniting Families Act
provides an 80,640 visa allocation for the unmarried sons and daughters of permanent residents
Document 9: Reuniting Families Act
amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants
Document 10: Border Security, Economic Opportunity, and Immigration Modernization Act
establishing a coherent and just system for integrating those who seek to join American society

topic.name: Transportation Safety and Funding
topic.description: The documents focus on the authorization and funding of various transportation safety programs and initiatives within the aviation and rail sectors.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) for FY2010 and FY2011 for operations.
Directs the FAA Administrator to establish a program to utilize undergraduate and technical colleges in research on subjects of relevance to the FAA.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize through FY2014 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs.
Increases the individual small business award levels for participation at the Phase I and Phase II levels.
Document 5: Requires the Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) to make safety in railroad transportation the highest priority.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation to develop a long-term railroad strategy for improving railroad safety.
Document 6: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the Coast Guard for operation and maintenance.
Research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies relating to the Coast Guard's mission in marine safety.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy in Iraq and Syria
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures and policies aimed at addressing security, stability, and humanitarian issues in Iraq and Syria.

Document 1: Requires that, no later than December 31, 2007, all U.S. Armed Forces serving in Iraq be redeployed outside of Iraq
Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should take a leadership role in diplomatic efforts and negotiations for the long-term stability of Iraq.
Document 2: Directs the President to impose U.S.-based property blocking and entry sanctions against any foreign person that threatens the peace, security, or stability of Iraq or Syria
It is the sense of Congress that the government of Syria is in violation of numerous U.N. Security Council Resolutions regarding the provision of humanitarian assistance to the people of Syria.
Document 3: Authorizes the President to utilize the Proliferation Security Initiative and other measures to enforce all U.S. laws and Executive Orders

Requires the President to report periodically to Congress on the identity of all known countries that have engaged in the import into or export from Iran of items useful to any nuclear, biological, chemical, missile, or conventional arms program.
Document 4: States that U.S. sanctions, controls, and regulations relating to Syria shall remain in effect until the President certifies that Syria has ceased support for terrorism
Directs the President to provide assistance to support a democratic transition in Syria.
Document 5: Directs the President to implement a regional diplomatic plan in conjunction with the U.N. Security Council to curtail the ongoing civil war in Iraq
States that it is U.S. policy to undertake a sustained diplomatic effort to engage the governments of regional nations on issues that impact the situation in Iraq.
Document 6: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of State should redesignate North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 7: States that U.S. sanctions, controls, and regulations relating to Syria shall remain in effect until the President certifies that Syria has ceased support for terrorism
Sets forth diplomatic measures intended to isolate the government of Syria.
Document 8: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea until the President certifies to Congress that North Korea is no longer engaged in the illegal transfer of missile or nuclear technology
Directs the President to instruct the U.S. Permanent Representative to the United Nations to use U.S. influence to secure adoption of a Security Council resolution condemning North Korea's action.
Document 9: Expresses the sense of Congress that Congress should vote on a new authorization for use of force resolution that reflects the current Iraq situation
The United States should intensify diplomatic relations to support the difficult internal steps that the government of Iraq should take to promote national reconciliation.
Document 10: Maintains U.S. restrictions against Syria until the President certifies that Syria has ceased support for terrorism

Imposes specified trade, assistance, admissions, and military sanctions, as appropriate, on persons or countries that transfer goods or technology contributing to Syria's biological, chemical, nuclear, or advanced conventional weapons programs.

topic.name: Tax Reform and Simplification
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative proposals aimed at reforming and simplifying the U.S. tax code, focusing on changes to income tax rates, deductions, and credits.

1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the taxation of individual and married taxpayers
reduce to two the number of individual income tax brackets, imposing a 15% tax rate on income up to $87,850
2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 19 percent
Redefines 'taxable income' to mean the amount by which wages, retirement distributions, and unemployment compensation exceed the standard deduction
3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 17%
Increases the basic standard deduction and includes an additional standard deduction for dependents
4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow in 2008 and 2009: (1) an increase in the basic standard tax deduction
Provides for certain revenue-raising provisions, including: (1) treatment of net income and loss from an investment services partnership as ordinary income
5: Extends through 2013 for a taxpayer whose income is $200,000 or less the tax rate reductions and other tax benefits
Modifies estate tax provisions to establish: (1) an estate tax exclusion of $3.5 million
6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 17%
Repeals: (1) the alternative minimum tax; (2) all income tax credits; (3) estate, gift, and generation-skipping transfer taxes
7: Repeals, after 2006, income tax and estate and gift tax provisions of the Internal Revenue Code
Establishes three income tax brackets at 15, 25, and 30% for individual taxpayers
8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to replace the marginal income tax rates with a single rate of 17%
Redefines 'taxable income' to mean the amount by which wages, retirement distributions, and unemployment compensation exceed the standard deduction
9: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to individual income tax rates
Increases and modifies: (1) the standard tax deduction for individual taxpayers
10: Extends through 2013 for an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income is less than the applicable threshold amount the tax rate reductions
Modifies individual income tax brackets for 2013 to reduce income tax for taxpayers whose adjusted gross income is less than the applicable threshold amount

topic.name: Immigration and Naturalization Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures and acts related to immigration, naturalization, and the treatment of foreign nationals in the United States.

Document 1: period spent by an alien working as an interpreter or translator for naturalization purposes
Document 5: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to limit citizenship at birth
Document 9: increases criminal penalties for certain removed aliens who illegally reenter the United States
Document 10: provides for the loss of U.S. nationality by being an unprivileged enemy belligerent

topic.name: Forensic Science Research and Standards
topic.description: The documents discuss federal initiatives aimed at enhancing research, coordination, and standards in forensic science.

Document 1: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.
Directs NSF to award forensic science basic research grants to improve the foundation and practice of forensic science in the United States.
Document 3: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.
Directs NSF to create a merit-reviewed, competitive forensic science research grants program to improve the foundation and practice of forensic science in the United States.

topic.name: Alternative Fuel Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on promoting the use of alternative fuels and vehicles, particularly natural gas and ethanol, through tax credits and infrastructure development.

Document 1: 10% of new vehicles sold in the United States should be natural gas vehicles by December 31, 2018.
Requires each retail automotive fueling station owned by a major integrated oil company to have at least one pump dispensing natural gas for automotive purposes by January 1, 2018.
Document 2: Requires automobile manufacturers to ensure that at least 50% of 2012 and 2013 model year automobiles and light duty trucks manufactured for sale in the United States are dual fueled.
Requires the Secretary of Energy to make grants to eligible facilities to pay the federal share of installing blender pump fuel infrastructure.
Document 3: Allow an excise tax credit through 2016 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the EPA should streamline the process for certification of natural gas vehicle retrofit kits.
Document 4: Requires automobile manufacturers to ensure that at least 50% of 2013 and 2014 model year automobiles and light duty trucks manufactured for sale in the United States are dual fueled.
Requires the Secretary of Energy to make grants to eligible facilities to pay the federal share of installing blender pump fuel infrastructure.
Document 5: Increases the volume of renewable fuel that gasoline is required to contain for 2008-2012.
Redefines the term 'renewable fuel' to include motor vehicle fuel that is derived from renewable biomass.
Document 6: Allow an excise tax credit through 2018 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas.
Requires the Secretary of Energy to provide funding to improve the performance, efficiency, and integration of natural gas powered motor vehicles.
Document 7: Requires each manufacturer to ensure that at least 50% of 2011 and 2012 model year automobiles and light duty trucks manufactured for sale in the United States are dual fueled.
Provides for infrastructure installation subgrants to direct retailers of ethanol fuel blends.
Document 8: Extends through 2014 the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources.
Authorizes appropriations for advanced biofuels research, development, and demonstration.
Document 9: Allow an excise tax credit through 2019 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas.
Requires federal agencies to purchase dedicated alternative fuel vehicles for their fleets.
Document 10: Allow an excise tax credit through 2027 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas.
Requires 50% of all new vehicles purchased or placed in service by the U.S. government by December 31, 2014, to be capable of operating on compressed or liquefied natural gas.

topic.name: Banking and Mortgage Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating banking practices and consumer mortgage transactions to ensure financial stability and protect consumers.

Document 1: Establishes a framework for a market-based trigger to monitor and regulate the adequacy of bank capital
Subjects to receivership a bank holding company that... fails to submit an action plan required under this Act.
Document 2: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act

Prohibits the Board from requiring a nonbank financial company that is an insurance company under its supervision to comply with accounting standards that differ from regulatory accounting standards under state law.
Document 3: Prohibits a creditor or mortgage broker from arranging a consumer credit transaction secured by the principal dwelling of a consumer without first verifying the consumer's reasonable ability to make the scheduled payments
Limits finance points, charges, and fees.
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees any compensation paid by a mortgage originator or a creditor.
Document 5: Prohibits a mortgage broker or creditor from... steering, counseling, or directing a consumer to rates, charges, principal amount, or prepayment terms that are more expensive than those for which the consumer qualifies
Defines 'special mortgage' as one that...
Document 6: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to exempt from its rules regarding management assessment of internal controls the following institutions
Requires the CFPB... to provide by regulation that a 'qualified mortgage' includes a balloon loan extended by an insured depository institution.
Document 7: Modifies the criteria for exclusion from the computation of points and fees of certain reasonable charges elsewhere exempted from the computation of the finance charge
Excludes from points and fees any such reasonable charges even though a creditor receives compensation.
Document 8: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees any escrow for future payment of insurance.
Document 9: Excludes from points and fees any such reasonable charges even though a creditor receives compensation
Modifies the criteria for exclusion from the computation of points and fees.
Document 10: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Excludes from the computation of such points and fees any escrow for future payment of insurance.

topic.name: Land Trusts and Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the conveyance of land to Native American tribes and the designation of wilderness areas.

Document 1: approximately 26,565 acres of land in Nevada... held in trust for the benefit of the Moapa Band of Paiutes
Requires the Tribe to use the land for traditional and customary uses
Document 2: amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to designate certain federal lands... as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
approximately 6,349 acres identified as the Proposed Ute Pass Wilderness
Document 3: Designates the following lands in California as wilderness:... Cleveland National Forest, to be part of the Agua Tibia Wilderness
Provides for access to the Cahuilla Mountain Wilderness by Indian tribes for traditional cultural purposes
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to accept title to any additional number of acres of real property... if such real property is conveyed or otherwise transferred to the United States by or on behalf of the Tribe
all real property taken into trust within those boundaries shall be part of the Tribe's reservation
Document 7: holds in trust for the Confederated Tribes of Coos, Lower Umpqua, and Siuslaw Indians all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to approximately 14,804 acres of federal land
Makes that land part of the Tribes' reservation
Document 9: Directs the Department of the Interior to convey to Elko County... approximately 275 acres of land managed by the Bureau of Land Management
Declares that the United States holds approximately 373 acres of BLM administered land in trust for the Te-moak Tribe of Western Shoshone Indians of Nevada

topic.name: Middle School Improvement Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative efforts to improve educational outcomes for middle school students, particularly those from low-income backgrounds.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to make matching grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17, to implement state middle school improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a majority of middle grade students matriculate to high schools with graduation rates below 60%.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states and local educational agencies to implement reforms and innovations designed to improve educational outcomes significantly.
Gives grant priority to LEAs with the highest number or percentages of impoverished children.
Document 3: Directs the Department of Education to allot grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17, to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Priority is given to LEAs that serve high proportions of poor children.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a majority of middle grade students are not rated proficient on required state assessments.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Education to allot grants to states to cover part or all of the Advanced Placement or International Baccalaureate examination fee low-income students incur.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary to award renewable competitive matching grants to states for activities that increase the number of qualified teachers at high-need schools.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a majority of middle grade students are not rated proficient on required state assessments.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Favors LEAs that serve high proportions of poor children.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a majority of middle grade students are not rated proficient on required state assessments.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.
Gives priority to LEAs that serve high proportions of poor children.

topic.name: Tax and Housing Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to taxation, unemployment benefits, and housing finance reforms aimed at supporting individuals and families.

Document 1: Makes permanent the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 for individual taxpayers
increases the top marginal estate tax rate from 35% to 40%.
Document 2: extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until May 31, 2014.
Document 3: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor to exclude from the computation of debts the secured or unsecured portions of debts secured by the debtor's principal residence
Requires the court to disallow a claim that is subject to any remedy for rescission under the Truth in Lending Act.
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to prescribe fiduciary standards for originators of residential mortgages
Prohibits steering incentives in connection with mortgage loan origination.
Document 5: Directs the Federal Mortgage Insurance Corporation (FMIC) to take all steps necessary to dissolve and eliminate Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac
establishes the FMIC as an independent agency and instrumentality of the federal government.
Document 6: extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before June 1, 2014
Amends the FSEUCA of 1970 to postpone termination of the period during which a state may determine its 'on' and 'off' indicators.
Document 7: modifies requirements governing the maximum principal loan obligation
revises eligibility criteria for cash down payment for mortgage insurance.
Document 8: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to revise requirements for public housing agency inspections
Requires an initial inspection before any assistance payment is made.
Document 9: modifies requirements for the maximum principal loan obligation
reduces such payment from the current 3% to 1.5% of the appraised value of the property.
Document 10: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 with respect to guaranteed rural housing loans to require mortgagees to engage in loss mitigation actions
Grants the Secretary authority to establish a program for assignment to HUD of a guaranteed mortgage.

topic.name: Post Office Building Designations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the legislative measures that designate various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 7802 37th Avenue in Jackson Heights, New York, as the 'Jeanne and Jules Manford Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 55 South Pioneer Boulevard in Springboro, Ohio, as the 'Richard 'Dick' Chenault Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 90 Vermilyea Avenue, in New York, New York as the 'Corporal Juan Mariel Alcantara Post Office Building.'
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 198 Baker Street in Corning, New York, as the 'Specialist Ryan P. Jayne Post Office Building.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1335 Jefferson Road in Rochester, New York, as the 'Specialist Theodore Matthew Glende Post Office.'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 10360 Southwest 186th Street in Miami, Florida, as the 'Larcenia J. Bullard Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 523 East Railroad Street in Knox, Pennsylvania, as the 'Specialist Ross A. McGinnis Memorial Post Office.'
Document 8: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 220 Elm Avenue in Munising, Michigan, as the 'Elizabeth L. Kinnunen Post Office Building.'
Document 9: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 100 Admiral Callaghan Lane in Vallejo, California, as the 'Philmore Graham Post Office Building.'
Document 10: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 442 Miller Valley Road in Prescott, Arizona, as the 'Barry M. Goldwater Post Office.'

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at providing financial assistance, healthcare, and support for veterans and their families, particularly relating to disabilities and transition from military to civilian life.

Document 1: Wounded Warrior Transition Assistance Act of 2009 - Directs the Secretary of each military department to provide each reserve member of that department who is being evaluated for separation or retirement for disability...
Requires military human resources commands to return to active duty during their evaluation period reserve members who request such option.
Document 2: Increases the authorized allowance for burial and funeral expenses for deceased veterans...
Increases the burial plot allowance for veterans who, at the time of death, were receiving hospital or nursing home care...
Document 3: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation with respect to any service-connected disability...

Makes eligible for the full concurrent receipt of both veterans' disability compensation and either military retired pay or combat-related special pay those individuals who were retired or separated from military service due to a service-connected disability.
Document 4: Requires a member of the Armed Forces who is medically retired because of physical disability incurred in or aggravated by military service to be treated as an active-duty member...

Directs the Secretaries of Defense and Veterans Affairs to establish a joint office for the Department of Defense (DOD) and VA for the coordination of assistance to members in their transition from military service to civilian life.
Document 5: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to revise its requirements for exigency leave with respect to employees belonging to the family of members of the Armed Forces...
Entitles to coverage by the Act an eligible employee who is a family member caring for a veteran undergoing medical treatment, recuperation, or therapy for a serious injury or illness...
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to assign to that member a 30% temporary disability rating upon such member's separation from the Armed Forces...
States that disability compensation payments received by a member who receives such temporary disability rating are not subject to offset...
Document 7: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation with respect to any service-connected disability...
Makes the 2004 through 2013 phase-in limitations for the concurrent receipt of such pay for qualified retirees with service-connected disabilities rated as total applicable.
Document 8: Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to revise its requirements for exigency leave with respect to employees belonging to the family of members of the Armed Forces...
Defines a veteran's serious injury or illness of a veteran to be one: incurred by the veteran as an Armed Forces member in the line of duty...
Document 9: This bill addresses medical evidence reviews in the case of a former member of the Armed Forces who was deployed in a contingency operation and subsequently diagnosed as suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder...
Any board of review shall review medical evidence of the Department of Veterans Affairs or a civilian health care provider presented by the former member.
Document 10: Entitles a surviving spouse to a benefit for the month of a veteran's death if, at the time of the veteran's death: the veteran was receiving veterans' disability compensation or veteran's pension...
Includes under the VA's presidential memorial certificate program an individual who dies during active service.

topic.name: Abortion Legislation for Minors
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. federal legislation regulating abortion access for minors, particularly concerning parental notification and involvement.

1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
requires parental involvement in the minor's abortion decision
3: Imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly or knowingly attempts to perform an abortion based on the sex or gender of the child
authorizes civil actions for verifiable money damages for injuries
4: Makes it an affirmative defense to a prosecution or civil action
Defines 'abortion' as the termination of a pregnancy with an intention other than to increase the probability of a live birth
5: Imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly or knowingly attempts to perform an abortion based on the sex, gender, color or race of the child
authorizes civil actions by fathers, or maternal grandparents if the mother is an unemancipated minor
6: Imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly or knowingly attempts to perform an abortion based on the sex, gender, color or race of the child
authorizes injunctive relief to be obtained by the women upon whom such an abortion is performed or attempted
7: deems such transporting to be a de facto abridgment of the right of a parent
imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to one year on a physician
8: Defines 'abortion' to mean the use or prescription of any instrument, medicine, drug, or any other substance or device
requires such physician to give 24-hour actual or constructive notice to a parent
9: Makes an exception for an abortion necessary to save the life of the minor
imposes a fine and/or prison term of up to one year on a physician who performs or induces an abortion
10: Defines 'abortion' to mean the use or prescription of any instrument, medicine, drug, or any other substance or device
requires such physician to give 24-hour actual or constructive notice to a parent

topic.name: Water and Land Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to water resource management, land conservation, and environmental protection in the United States.

Document 1: Water Resources Development Act of 2016
requires the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to enter cooperative agreements with youth service and conservation corps organizations for services on Corps projects.
Document 2: Designates certain lands in the Skykomish River Valley, Washington, as the Wild Sky Wilderness
requires the Secretary to establish a trail plan.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to encourage the use of U.S. workers and equipment manufactured in the U.S.
streamlining permitting of American energy.
Document 4: directs the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to submit a report including projects operated for flood control
carry out at least 15 pilot projects to implement revisions of operational documents.
Document 5: establish at least one Forest Reserve Revenue Area within each unit of the National Forest System
provide a dependable source of 25% payments and economic activity for each beneficiary county.
Document 6: Designates approximately 26,000 acres of land managed by the Bureau of Land Management as the Pine Forest Range Wilderness
allows livestock grazing already established in the Wilderness to continue.
Document 7: Amends the Colorado River Storage Project Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to create and operate a top water bank
construct, operate, and maintain the Navajo Indian Irrigation Project.
Document 8: Prohibits construction, operation, or maintenance of the oil pipeline unless it is in compliance with the terms of a permit
instructs the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to issue a permit within 30 days.
Document 9: establishes a process for declaration of a major disaster for wildfire on federal lands
the President shall establish a wildfire suppression operations account for wildfires on federal lands.
Document 10: Designates specified federal lands in the Monongahela National Forest as wilderness
requires the Secretary to develop a plan to provide for enhanced nonmotorized recreation trail opportunities.

topic.name: Tax Relief Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on amending the Internal Revenue Code to provide various forms of tax relief and deductions for individuals and families.

1: Universal Homeowners' Tax Relief Act of 2007
additional standard tax deduction for state and local real property taxes
2: exclusion from gross income of distributions from individual retirement accounts for charitable purposes
3: increase the limit on the deductibility of losses from the sale or exchange of capital assets
4: increase to $20,000 the limitation on the amount of losses from capital assets
5: deduction from the gross estate of a decedent for capital losses
eligibility to carry over to subsequent taxable years
6: tax deduction for qualified tuition and related expenses
7: increase the limit on the deductibility of losses
8: exemption from tax penalties for premature distributions
9: increase the limit on the deductibility of losses
10: Adoption Tax Credit Refundability Act of 2015
make the tax credit for adoption expenses refundable
